title: NodeSlide Reviewable Creation Demo
audience: product leaders evaluating an AI presentation workflow
decision: decide whether reviewable generation is materially safer than one-shot deck creation
prompt:
Create a seven-slide product demo for product leaders evaluating reviewable AI presentation generation. Show the initial brief, structured draft, validation, review-before-accept, trace receipt, editable export, and final decision. Use the supplied journey timestamps. Include a large screenshot or explicit screenshot placeholder with numbered callouts, a before/after comparison, an editable interaction flow, and a trace waterfall. Never claim a screenshot was captured when it was not supplied.

Design direction: Use an evidence-led editorial direction: restrained surface treatment, decisive hierarchy, visible provenance, and an inspectable visual argument on every proof slide. Avoid card grids and decorative dashboards.

human_accept,52,complete
pptx_export,58,complete
No browser screenshot is included in this frozen evidence pack. The deck must reserve and label the screenshot proof honestly rather than fabricating one.

designDirection.label: Evidence editorial
designDirection.instruction: Use an evidence-led editorial direction: restrained surface treatment, decisive hierarchy, visible provenance, and an inspectable visual argument on every proof slide. Avoid card grids and decorative dashboards.

trace.summary: The user consented to send the full brief and uploaded data sources to OpenRouter. NodeSlide used its deterministic fallback because The Gemini 3.5 Flash via OpenRouter route had no compatible provider endpoint. Self-critique: 1 pass (deterministic route; self-critique loop skipped).
